title: High quality DNA from Fusarium oxysporum conidia for PacBio sequencing
author: Sukhwinder Kaur
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.i8ichue
materials: Sterilize all dry supplies by autoclaving.

Air dry the pellet for 15 to 20 min until there is no visible ethanol residue. Add 100 ul of EB (10mM Tris pH 8.5, Qiagen). Incubate at RT overnight. Next morning, incubate at 28 C on a platform orbit shaker at 150 rpm for 1 h.

steps.title: Spread a Fusarium oxysporum conidial suspension...
steps.instruction: Spread a Fusarium oxysporum conidial suspension onto 10 to 20, 100-mm-diam Petri dishes with potato dextrose agar. The goal is to obtain ca. 0.5 ml packed conidial volume, which yields ca. 250 mg lyophilized conidia.

steps.title: Use 1 ml of freshly prepared...
steps.instruction: Use 1 ml of freshly prepared extraction buffer with RNase A per 50 mg of lyophilized Fusarium oxysporum conidia. Prepare fresh just before extraction For 5 ml extraction buffer add sequentially: 4 ml Milli-Q nuclease-free water 1 ml 5xRNB 240 mg PAS (Sodium 4-aminosalicylate dihydrate). Mix and then vortex until dissolved. 40 mg TIPS (Triisopropylnaphthalenesulfonic acid sodium) Mix and then vortex until dissolved. Wait until the foam disappears. Add 5µl (50 µg) of RNase A (10mg/ml stock)

steps.title: Cool a mortar (9 cm upper...
steps.instruction: Cool a mortar (9 cm upper diam) with liquid N 2 .

steps.title: Produce conidia by incubating dishes under fluorescent lights at ca
steps.instruction: Produce conidia by incubating dishes under fluorescent lights at ca. 25ºC for 6 to 7 days. 144:00:00, Incubation

steps.title: To harvest the conidia, from each...
steps.instruction: To harvest the conidia, from each dish, pour 5 ml water on the culture, and gently rub the culture with a metal “hockey stick.” Repeat conidial collection with an additional 5 ml water.

steps.title: Pour the dislodged conidia through two...
steps.instruction: Pour the dislodged conidia through two layers of Miracloth into 50 ml tubes.

steps.title: Centrifuge the conidia at 4 °C...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the conidia at 4 °C at 3620 g for 15 min. 00:15:00, Centrifugation

steps.title: Remove the supernatant (SN), resuspend and re-pellet
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant (SN), resuspend and re-pellet.

steps.title: Suspend the pellet in 3 to 5 ml water
steps.instruction: Suspend the pellet in 3 to 5 ml water.

steps.title: Transfer the conidial suspensions in 1...
steps.instruction: Transfer the conidial suspensions in 1 ml aliquots, each in a 2 ml microfuge tube. The goal is ca. 50 mg conidia dry wt/tube.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 3620 g for 15 min
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 3620 g for 15 min. 00:15:00, Centrifugation

steps.title: Remove water, freeze the conidial pellet...
steps.instruction: Remove water, freeze the conidial pellet at -70 ºC overnight (ON) and lyophilize it dry. Lyophilized conidia can be stored at -70 °C before grinding and extraction.

steps.title: Add additional liquid N 2 to cool the sand
steps.instruction: Add additional liquid N 2 to cool the sand.

steps.title: Grind quickly with force for 15 to 20 sec
steps.instruction: Grind quickly with force for 15 to 20 sec. (1/3) 00:00:20, Grinding

steps.title: Add more liquid N 2
steps.instruction: Add more liquid N 2 .

steps.title: Transfer the ground conidia and sand...
steps.instruction: Transfer the ground conidia and sand mixture to a 2 ml microfuge tube (T#1) containing 1 ml of extraction buffer with RNase A from step 12. To optimize your grinding procedure, examine ground lyophilized conidia microscopically (Fig. 1); you need to grind sufficiently so that most of the conidia are cracked open. Any more grinding than that only shears the DNA.] We typically prepare 4 tubes, each with 50 mg lyophilized conidia per preparation. The tubes are pooled at the end of the preparation.

steps.title: Label 3 additional tubes (T#2, T#3,...
steps.instruction: Label 3 additional tubes (T#2, T#3, & T#4) per 50 mg conidia.

steps.title: Mix the T#1 tube from step...
steps.instruction: Mix the T#1 tube from step 31 on a horizontal low speed (200 rpm) vortex mixer for 2 min. 00:02:00, Vortex

steps.title: Incubate at room temperature (RT) for...
steps.instruction: Incubate at room temperature (RT) for 30 min, with mixing by inverting tubes every 5 min. 00:30:00, Incubation at RT 00:05:00, Inverting

steps.title: Add 6 µl (120 µg) of proteinase K to T#1
steps.instruction: Add 6 µl (120 µg) of proteinase K to T#1.

steps.title: Mix well on the low speed...
steps.instruction: Mix well on the low speed horizontal vortex mixer for 20 min at RT. 00:20:00, Vortex

steps.title: Cool tubes on ice for 5 min
steps.instruction: Cool tubes on ice for 5 min. 00:05:00, Cooling on ice

steps.title: Add an equal volume of ice-cold, buffered P:C:I (25:24:1)
steps.instruction: Add an equal volume of ice-cold, buffered P:C:I (25:24:1)

steps.title: Mix on the horizontal vortex mixer for 15 sec
steps.instruction: Mix on the horizontal vortex mixer for 15 sec. 00:00:15, Vortex

steps.title: Transfer supernatant (SN) to a new...
steps.instruction: Transfer supernatant (SN) to a new 2 ml microfuge tube (T#2).

steps.title: Add an equal volume of ice-cold, buffered P:C:I
steps.instruction: Add an equal volume of ice-cold, buffered P:C:I.

steps.title: Mix on the horizontal vortex mixer...
steps.instruction: Mix on the horizontal vortex mixer for 2 min at 4ºC. 00:02:00, Vortex Always avoid transferring any of the interfaces.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 15000g for 10 min at 4ºC
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 15000g for 10 min at 4ºC. 00:10:00, Centrifugation

steps.title: Transfer SN (Vol = 800 to...
steps.instruction: Transfer SN (Vol = 800 to 900µl) to a new 2 ml tube (T#3).

steps.title: Add an equal volume of ice-cold chloroform:isoamyl alcohol (C:I) (24:1)
steps.instruction: Add an equal volume of ice-cold chloroform:isoamyl alcohol (C:I) (24:1).

steps.title: Mix on the horizontal vortex mixer...
steps.instruction: Mix on the horizontal vortex mixer for 15 sec at 4ºC. 00:00:15, Vortex

steps.title: Centrifuge at 15000g for 5 min at 4°C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 15000g for 5 min at 4°C. 00:05:00, Centrifugation

steps.title: Very carefully, remove the SN without...
steps.instruction: Very carefully, remove the SN without disturbing the interface to a new 2 ml tube (T#4).

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.1 volume of ice-cold 4M NaCl to T#4 and mix by inverting the tube several times. DNA strings should be visible.

steps.title: Add 1 volume of ice-cold isopropyl alcohol (IPA)
steps.instruction: Add 1 volume of ice-cold isopropyl alcohol (IPA).

steps.title: Mix well by inverting tubes gently at least 20 times
steps.instruction: Mix well by inverting tubes gently at least 20 times.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 10,000g for 30 min at 4ºC
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 10,000g for 30 min at 4ºC. 00:30:00, Centrifugation

steps.title: Centrifuge at 15000g for 6 min at 4ºC
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 15000g for 6 min at 4ºC. 00:06:00, Centrifugation The buffer is on top. Only use the lower PCI, here and in the subsequent step.

steps.title: Incubate on ice for 10 min
steps.instruction: Incubate on ice for 10 min. 00:10:00, Incubation on ice

steps.title: Discard the SN
steps.instruction: Discard the SN. Wash the pellet with 1.5 ml ice-cold 70% ethanol. Centrifuge at 13000 g for 5 min at RT.

steps.title: Discard the SN
steps.instruction: Discard the SN. Wash the pellet with 1.5 ml ice-cold 70% ethanol. Centrifuge at 13000 g for 5 min at RT. Discard SN and centrifuge for 1 min at 13000 g at RT. Remove residual ethanol with a fine Pippettor tip (e.g. P10 or P20).

steps.title: Determine the DNA concentration with a...
steps.instruction: Determine the DNA concentration with a Nanodrop (ND) and by Qubit. The Nanodrop 260/280 ratio should be 1.8-1.9 and the 260/230 ratio should be >2. Based on Qubit, the DNA quantity should be >200ng/ul. At this point, the concentrations of Qubit /ND is often ca. 0.1. [Note: further cleanup is required until the Qubit/ND ratio >0.7.]

steps.title: Perform an AMpure bead cleanup using either a 0
steps.instruction: Perform an AMpure bead cleanup using either a 0.45X or 0.5X volume of beads as indicated below. Elute in 50 to100 ul EB.

steps.title: Based on Qubit, the DNA concentration should be >100ng/ul
steps.instruction: Based on Qubit, the DNA concentration should be >100ng/ul. The size of the DNA should be checked by gel electrophoresis. We typically yield 80 ug DNA with mol wt. >48 kb per 250 mg lyophilized conidia.

steps.title: Bring AMpure beads suspension to RT
steps.instruction: Bring AMpure beads suspension to RT. Pipet out the beads slowly. Add 0.45X or 0.5X volume of the AMpure beads to the DNA.

steps.title: Mix the beads in the DNA...
steps.instruction: Mix the beads in the DNA solution thoroughly by flicking the tube. Do not vortex.

steps.title: Collect but do not pellet the...
steps.instruction: Collect but do not pellet the beads at 7,000 g for 2 to 3 sec.

steps.title: Allow the DNA to bind to...
steps.instruction: Allow the DNA to bind to the beads by shaking at 200 rpm on a horizontal vortex mixer for 10 min.

steps.title: Incubate the tubes on a rotating...
steps.instruction: Incubate the tubes on a rotating wheel at 22 rpm for 20 min.

steps.title: Centrifuge tubes at 7000 g for...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge tubes at 7000 g for 10 sec to pellet the beads.

steps.title: Place the tubes in a magnetic...
steps.instruction: Place the tubes in a magnetic rack to hold the pellet on the side of the tube.

steps.title: Slowly pipette off the SN
steps.instruction: Slowly pipette off the SN. As a precautionary measure, save the SN in a new tube, but only as a backup–your DNA should be bound to the beads. Avoid disturbing the bead pellet.

steps.title: With the beads in the magnetic...
steps.instruction: With the beads in the magnetic rack, add 70% ethanol to the side of tube, i.e., avoid the pellet. Fill the 70% ethanol to the rim of the tube.10.Pipette off the ethanol and wash again with 70% ethanol as above. Remove all the ethanol without disturbing the bead pellet.

steps.title: Remove tubes from the magnetic rack...
steps.instruction: Remove tubes from the magnetic rack and centrifuge briefly at 7000 g for 30 sec to collect the residual ethanol. Place tubes on the magnetic rack and remove the ethanol with a fine tip Pipette (P20 or P10).

steps.title: Check for any ethanol droplets
steps.instruction: Check for any ethanol droplets. If present, repeat the previous step.

steps.title: Remove the tubes from the magnetic rack
steps.instruction: Remove the tubes from the magnetic rack. Open the caps. Add the amount of EB for >100ng/ul DNA (by Qubit). Mix the EB and beads gently by flicking until the suspension is homogenous. Centrifuge the tubes briefly at 7000 g for 30 sec. Place the tubes in the magnetic rack and let the DNA elute off the beads for 5 to 10 minutes. When all the brown beads are on the side with the magnet, carefully and slowly remove the gDNA into a clean 1.5 ml microfuge tube. Do not pipet out any of the magnetic beads.

steps.title: Quantify DNA concentration and quality on Nanodrop and Qubit
steps.instruction: Quantify DNA concentration and quality on Nanodrop and Qubit. For sufficient quality, with Nanodrop, the 260/280 ratio should be between 1.7 to 1.9 and the 260/230 ratio should be higher than 2.0. The Qubit/Nanodrop concentration ratio should be > 0.7X.
